reasoning_summary: Net benefit after costs ranges from $2k to $26k per month, yielding a payback period between ~7 and 90 months. To meet the 12‑month payback requirement, monthly net benefit must be at least $15k, implying a revenue uplift of ~5.6% (within the 4‑7% forecast). Approval is conditional on achieving at least a 5.6% uplift.
key_assumptions: Revenue uplift applies to the full current monthly revenue of $2M
Infrastructure and maintenance costs are fixed at $30k per month
Implementation cost is incurred upfront and not amortized
Payback period calculated after the 3‑month implementation lag

most_likely_causes: Concept drift due to changed fraud patterns after promotional campaign
Introduction of a new payment channel altering feature space
evidence: Precision dropped from 0.82 to 0.61 and recall from 0.76 to 0.72
Feature distribution for transaction_amount shifted significantly
Fraud patterns changed after a promotional campaign
A new payment channel was introduced
Data pipeline logs show no failed jobs
less_likely_causes: Threshold miscalibration (no direct evidence of threshold change)
Pipeline failure (logs indicate all jobs succeeded)
recommended_diagnostics: Compute Population Stability Index (PSI) and Kolmogorov‑Smirnov test for transaction_amount and other key features across pre‑deployment vs. current data
Run drift detection per payment channel (e.g., ADWIN, DDM) to isolate impact of the new channel
Analyze label distribution and fraud pattern statistics over time to detect concept drift
Perform a hold‑out evaluation using recent labeled data to quantify performance loss per segment
Check calibration curves and precision‑recall trade‑off to see if threshold adjustment could recover precision
short_term_actions: Temporarily adjust the decision threshold to improve precision while monitoring recall
Enable per‑channel monitoring dashboards and set alerts for sudden drops in precision/recall
Run an offline re‑training on the most recent 1‑month data and evaluate before production rollout
If feasible, route transactions from the new payment channel through a fallback rule‑based filter pending model update
long_term_actions: Implement automated drift detection pipelines that trigger retraining or model review when PSI/KS exceeds predefined limits
Establish a regular (e.g., monthly) model retraining schedule incorporating new fraud patterns and payment channels
Add the new payment channel as an explicit feature and continuously monitor its importance
Adopt online or incremental learning methods to adapt to evolving fraud behavior
Maintain a calibration monitoring service to detect threshold misalignment over time
reasoning_summary: The sharp decline in precision, together with a significant shift in the transaction_amount feature and the introduction of a new payment channel, points to data drift. Concurrently, the promotional campaign altered fraud tactics, indicating concept drift. Pipeline failures are ruled out by clean logs, and there is no evidence of intentional threshold changes, making miscalibration less likely. Diagnostics focus on quantifying feature and label drift, while short‑term actions aim to mitigate immediate precision loss and isolate the new channel's impact. Long‑term actions establish continuous monitoring and scheduled retraining to prevent recurrence.
